description: Agent coordination manifest - claims prevent conflicts

changes: Added _find_fork_bomb_unescaped() to catch fork bombs with escaped chars that bypass lowercase regex
Extended _DANGEROUS_ENV_VARS to include dyld_library_path (macOS DLL injection vector)
Enhanced _blocked_curl_wget() to detect silent fetch patterns and config/header injection
Fixed dangerous env var detection to trigger for PATH= anywhere in command, not just after env prefix
Added _blocked_sudo_n() to block sudo -n (non-interactive password attempt) and sudo -E
security_notes: Fork bomb bypass: original regex worked on lowercased normalized string, but \: \| \& escapes prevent lowercase from matching - now unescape before regex
macOS persistence: dyld_library_path mirrors ld_preload on Darwin for DYLD injection
Curl silently fetching remote content to shell is dangerous - added silent fetch detection
PATH= assignment anywhere in command can hijack command resolution

changes: Added session_start/session_end event logging with log_session_start(), log_session_end()
Added agent_id parameter to log_tool_call() for tracking which agent made the call
Added policy_change audit event type via log_policy_change()
Added ensure_session_start_logged() to auto-capture session start on first tool call
audit_improvements: Session lifecycle now has explicit audit events (session_start, session_end)
Agent attribution now possible via optional agent_id parameter
Policy changes (created, updated, deleted, enabled, disabled) are now auditable
